description: The new Indian Evidence Act governing admissibility of evidence in courts from July 1, 2024.

sections.title: Electronic Records as Primary Evidence (replaces IEA 65B)
sections.text: Nothing shall apply in respect to the admissibility of any electronic record which is produced as a primary evidence under Section 57. Electronic records shall have the same legal effect, validity and enforceability as any other document.
sections.simplified: Digital records (emails, server logs, smartphone data, messages) are legally given the exact same status as physical paper documents.

sections.title: Admissibility of electronic records
sections.text: Any information contained in an electronic record which is printed on paper, stored, recorded or copied in optical or magnetic media or semiconductor memory produced by a computer or any communication device, shall be deemed to be a document.
sections.simplified: Clarifies that printouts from computers or data in hard drives/phones are admissible as long as the source and generation conditions are certified.

sections.title: Definition of Document
sections.text: 'Document' means any matter expressed or described upon any substance by means of letters, figures or marks, or by more than one of those means, intended to be used, or which may be used, for the purpose of recording that matter, and includes an electronic and digital record.
sections.simplified: The definition of documents is officially expanded to explicitly include any digital or electronic record.
